Records.authors: Marzucca, Robin, McLeod, Andrew J., Page, Ben, Pögel, Sebastian, Weinzierl, Stefan
Records.description: Two-loop nonplanar crossed box with 7 equal-mass internal propagators and 4 massless external legs. Maximal cut in $d=4$ defines a genus 3 hyperelliptic curve that drops to genus 2 via an extra involution symmetry.

Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Porkert, Franziska, Semper, Cathrin, Stawinski, Sven F.
Records.description: Two-loop non-planar crossed box integral in $D=4-2\varepsilon$ dimensions with seven propagators all carrying equal mass $m$ (set to $m^2=1$ without loss of generality) and four massless external legs. Depends on two Mandelstam variables $s=(p_1+p_2)^2$, $t=(p_2+p_3)^2$. Previously studied in refs. [1308.6676, 1504.08258, 2306.05460] and connected to a genus-two hyperelliptic curve. Using the loop-by-loop Baikov approach, the maximal cut twist is $\Phi_{npcb} = [(z-\lambda_1)(z-\lambda_2)]^{-1/2} \cdot [(z-\lambda_3)(z-\lambda_4)(z-\lambda_5)(z-\lambda_6)]^{-1/2-\varepsilon}$, where the six roots $\lambda_i$ are algebraic functions of $s,t$. The first factor has NO $\varepsilon$-dependence in the exponent. At $\varepsilon=0$, the twist defines a hyperelliptic curve of genus two. The twisted cohomology group is 5-dimensional; the basis consists of differentials of the first kind ($i=1,2$), second kind ($i=3,4$), and third kind ($i=5$) on the hyperelliptic curve. The TRBRs at leading order in $\varepsilon$ yield generalized Legendre relations (Riemann bilinear relations on the genus-2 curve) relating a- and b-periods and quasi-periods.

Records.authors: Fevola, C., Mizera, S., Telen, S.
Records.description: Landau singularities of the non-planar double box computed via principal Landau determinants.
